question_text: You are migrating your on-premises data warehouse to BigQuery. As part of the migration, you want to facilitate cross-team collaboration to get the most value out of the organization’s data. You need to design an architecture that would allow teams within the organization to securely publish, discover, and subscribe to read-only data in a self-service manner. You need to minimize costs while also maximizing data freshness. What should you do?
choice_A: Use Analytics Hub to facilitate data sharing.
choice_B: Create authorized datasets to publish shared data in the subscribing team's project.
choice_C: Create a new dataset for sharing in each individual team’s project. Grant the subscribing team the bigquery.dataViewer role on the dataset.
choice_D: Use BigQuery Data Transfer Service to copy datasets to a centralized BigQuery project for sharing.
explanation: La réponse correcte est A. Utiliser Analytics Hub pour faciliter le partage de données. Analytics Hub est une solution de Google Cloud conçue spécifiquement pour le partage de données sécurisé et en libre-service entre équipes. Contrairement aux options B et C, qui nécessitent la gestion manuelle des autorisations et la duplication potentielle des données, Analytics Hub permet aux équipes de publier, découvrir et s'abonner à des ensembles de données de manière centralisée et efficace. De plus, l'option D, qui implique l'utilisation de BigQuery Data Transfer Service, pourrait entraîner des coûts supplémentaires et des retards dans la mise à jour des données, ce qui n'est pas optimal pour maximiser la fraîcheur des données.

question_text: You want to migrate an Apache Spark 3 batch job from on-premises to Google Cloud. You need to minimally change the job so that the job reads from Cloud Storage and writes the result to BigQuery. Your job is optimized for Spark, where each executor has 8 vCPU and 16 GB memory, and you want to be able to choose similar settings. You want to minimize installation and management effort to run your job. What should you do?
choice_A: Execute the job as part of a deployment in a new Google Kubernetes Engine cluster.
choice_B: Execute the job from a new Compute Engine VM.
choice_C: Execute the job in a new Dataproc cluster.
choice_D: Execute as a Dataproc Serverless job.
explanation: La réponse correcte est D. Exécuter en tant que tâche Dataproc Serverless. Dataproc Serverless permet d'exécuter des tâches Spark sans avoir à gérer l'infrastructure sous-jacente, ce qui minimise les efforts d'installation et de gestion. Contrairement aux options A et B, qui nécessitent la gestion de clusters Kubernetes ou de machines virtuelles Compute Engine, Dataproc Serverless gère automatiquement les ressources nécessaires. De plus, il permet de configurer les ressources de calcul pour correspondre aux exigences de votre tâche Spark, comme les 8 vCPU et 16 Go de mémoire par exécuteur. Enfin, Dataproc Serverless facilite l'intégration avec Google Cloud Storage pour la lecture des données et BigQuery pour l'écriture des résultats.

question_text: You are configuring networking for a Dataflow job. The data pipeline uses custom container images with the libraries that are required for the transformation logic preinstalled. The data pipeline reads the data from Cloud Storage and writes the data to BigQuery. You need to ensure cost-effective and secure communication between the pipeline and Google APIs and services. What should you do?
choice_A: Disable external IP addresses from worker VMs and enable Private Google Access.
choice_B: Leave external IP addresses assigned to worker VMs while enforcing firewall rules.
choice_C: Disable external IP addresses and establish a Private Service Connect endpoint IP address.
choice_D: Enable Cloud NAT to provide outbound internet connectivity while enforcing firewall rules.
explanation: La réponse correcte est A. Désactiver les adresses IP externes des VM de travail et activer l'accès privé à Google. En désactivant les adresses IP externes, vous empêchez l'accès direct à Internet, ce qui améliore la sécurité. L'activation de l'accès privé à Google permet aux VM de communiquer de manière sécurisée et économique avec les API et services Google via des adresses IP internes. Les autres options, comme B et D, impliquent l'utilisation d'adresses IP externes ou de Cloud NAT, ce qui peut entraîner des coûts supplémentaires et des risques de sécurité. L'option C, bien qu'elle utilise Private Service Connect, n'est pas nécessaire pour ce scénario spécifique où l'accès privé à Google suffit.

question_text: You are using Workflows to call an API that returns a 1KB JSON response, apply some complex business logic on this response, wait for the logic to complete, and then perform a load from a Cloud Storage file to BigQuery. The Workflows standard library does not have sufficient capabilities to perform your complex logic, and you want to use Python's standard library instead. You want to optimize your workflow for simplicity and speed of execution. What should you do?
choice_A: Create a Cloud Composer environment and run the logic in Cloud Composer.
choice_B: Create a Dataproc cluster, and use PySpark to apply the logic on your JSON file.
choice_C: Invoke a Cloud Function instance that uses Python to apply the logic on your JSON file.
choice_D: Invoke a subworkflow in Workflows to apply the logic on your JSON file.
explanation: La réponse correcte est C. Invoquer une instance de Cloud Function qui utilise Python pour appliquer la logique sur votre fichier JSON. Cloud Functions est une solution serverless qui permet d'exécuter du code en réponse à des événements, ce qui est idéal pour appliquer une logique complexe sur une réponse JSON de manière rapide et efficace. Contrairement à Cloud Composer (option A) et Dataproc (option B), qui sont plus adaptés pour des tâches de traitement de données à grande échelle et peuvent introduire une latence inutile, Cloud Functions offre une exécution plus simple et plus rapide pour des tâches ponctuelles. L'option D, invoquer un sous-flux dans Workflows, n'est pas viable car Workflows ne supporte pas directement l'exécution de code Python complexe.

question_text: You are administering a BigQuery on-demand environment. Your business intelligence tool is submitting hundreds of queries each day that aggregate a large (50 TB) sales history fact table at the day and month levels. These queries have a slow response time and are exceeding cost expectations. You need to decrease response time, lower query costs, and minimize maintenance. What should you do?
choice_A: Build authorized views on top of the sales table to aggregate data at the day and month level.
choice_B: Enable BI Engine and add your sales table as a preferred table.
choice_C: Build materialized views on top of the sales table to aggregate data at the day and month level.
choice_D: Create a scheduled query to build sales day and sales month aggregate tables on an hourly basis.
explanation: La réponse correcte est C. Construire des vues matérialisées sur la table des ventes pour agréger les données au niveau journalier et mensuel. Les vues matérialisées pré-calculent et stockent les résultats des requêtes, ce qui permet d'améliorer considérablement le temps de réponse des requêtes ultérieures, car elles n'ont pas besoin de recalculer les agrégats à chaque fois. Cela réduit également les coûts de requête, car moins de données doivent être traitées. Contrairement aux vues autorisées (option A) qui ne stockent pas de données pré-calculées, les vues matérialisées sont optimisées pour les performances et les coûts. L'option D, bien que similaire, nécessiterait une maintenance plus fréquente et pourrait ne pas être aussi efficace que les vues matérialisées. L'option B, activer BI Engine, pourrait améliorer les performances, mais elle est plus adaptée pour des ensembles de données plus petits et ne réduirait pas autant les coûts.

question_text: You have several different unstructured data sources, within your on-premises data center as well as in the cloud. The data is in various formats, such as Apache Parquet and CSV. You want to centralize this data in Cloud Storage. You need to set up an object sink for your data that allows you to use your own encryption keys. You want to use a GUI-based solution. What should you do?
choice_A: Use BigQuery Data Transfer Service to move files into BigQuery.
choice_B: Use Storage Transfer Service to move files into Cloud Storage
choice_C: Use Dataflow to move files into Cloud Storage
choice_D: Use Cloud Data Fusion to move files into Cloud Storage.
explanation: La réponse correcte est D. Utiliser Cloud Data Fusion pour déplacer des fichiers vers Cloud Storage. Cloud Data Fusion est une solution basée sur une interface graphique qui permet de concevoir, construire et gérer des pipelines de données de manière intuitive. Elle prend en charge divers formats de données, y compris Apache Parquet et CSV, et permet l'intégration de clés de chiffrement personnalisées pour sécuriser les données. Contrairement à BigQuery Data Transfer Service (A), qui est spécifiquement conçu pour transférer des données vers BigQuery, et à Dataflow (C), qui nécessite généralement des compétences en programmation, Cloud Data Fusion offre une approche plus accessible et visuelle pour centraliser les données dans Cloud Storage. Storage Transfer Service (B) est principalement utilisé pour transférer des données entre différents emplacements de stockage, mais ne fournit pas la même flexibilité en termes de transformation et de gestion des clés de chiffrement que Cloud Data Fusion.

question_text: You are using BigQuery with a regional dataset that includes a table with the daily sales volumes. This table is updated multiple times per day. You need to protect your sales table in case of regional failures with a recovery point objective (RPO) of less than 24 hours, while keeping costs to a minimum. What should you do?
choice_A: Schedule a daily export of the table to a Cloud Storage dual or multi-region bucket.
choice_B: Schedule a daily copy of the dataset to a backup region.
choice_C: Schedule a daily BigQuery snapshot of the table.
choice_D: Modify ETL job to load the data into both the current and another backup region.
explanation: La réponse correcte est A. Planifier une exportation quotidienne de la table vers un bucket Cloud Storage en double ou multi-région. Exporter les données vers un bucket Cloud Storage multi-région garantit que les données sont stockées de manière redondante dans plusieurs régions, ce qui offre une protection contre les pannes régionales tout en respectant un objectif de point de récupération (RPO) de moins de 24 heures. Cette solution est généralement plus économique que de maintenir des copies de l'ensemble du dataset dans une autre région, comme proposé dans les choix B et D. De plus, les snapshots BigQuery (choix C) sont spécifiques à une région et ne fournissent pas la redondance géographique nécessaire en cas de panne régionale.

question_text: You are preparing an organization-wide dataset. You need to preprocess customer data stored in a restricted bucket in Cloud Storage. The data will be used to create consumer analyses. You need to follow data privacy requirements, including protecting certain sensitive data elements, while also retaining all of the data for potential future use cases. What should you do?
choice_A: Use the Cloud Data Loss Prevention API and Dataflow to detect and remove sensitive fields from the data in Cloud Storage. Write the filtered data in BigQuery.
choice_B: Use customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) to directly encrypt the data in Cloud Storage. Use federated queries from BigQuery. Share the encryption key by following the principle of least privilege.
choice_C: Use Dataflow and the Cloud Data Loss Prevention API to mask sensitive data. Write the processed data in BigQuery.
choice_D: Use Dataflow and Cloud KMS to encrypt sensitive fields and write the encrypted data in BigQuery. Share the encryption key by following the principle of least privilege.
explanation: La réponse correcte est C. Utiliser Dataflow et l'API Cloud Data Loss Prevention pour masquer les données sensibles et écrire les données traitées dans BigQuery. Cette approche permet de respecter les exigences de confidentialité des données en masquant les éléments sensibles, ce qui protège la vie privée des clients tout en conservant les données pour des analyses futures. Contrairement à l'option A, qui supprime les champs sensibles, le masquage préserve l'intégrité des données pour des cas d'utilisation ultérieurs. L'option B, qui utilise le chiffrement, ne traite pas spécifiquement le masquage des données sensibles, et l'option D, bien qu'elle chiffre les champs sensibles, ne répond pas aussi directement aux exigences de masquage pour l'analyse des consommateurs.

question_text: Your company is running their first dynamic campaign, serving different offers by analyzing real-time data during the holiday season. The data scientists are collecting terabytes of data that rapidly grows every hour during their 30-day campaign. They are using Google Cloud Dataflow to preprocess the data and collect the feature (signals) data that is needed for the machine learning model in Google Cloud Bigtable. The team is observing suboptimal performance with reads and writes of their initial load of 10 TB of data. They want to improve this performance while minimizing cost. What should they do?
choice_A: Redefine the schema by evenly distributing reads and writes across the row space of the table.
choice_B: The performance issue should be resolved over time as the site of the BigDate cluster is increased.
choice_C: Redesign the schema to use a single row key to identify values that need to be updated frequently in the cluster.
choice_D: Redesign the schema to use row keys based on numeric IDs that increase sequentially per user viewing the offers.
explanation: La réponse correcte est A. Redéfinir le schéma en répartissant uniformément les lectures et les écritures sur l'espace des lignes de la table est essentiel pour optimiser les performances dans Google Cloud Bigtable. Bigtable est conçu pour gérer de grandes quantités de données, mais il est sensible aux points chauds, qui se produisent lorsque trop de lectures ou d'écritures sont concentrées sur un petit sous-ensemble de lignes. En répartissant uniformément les accès, on évite ces points chauds et on améliore les performances globales. Les autres options, comme B, qui suggère que le problème se résoudra avec le temps, ou C et D, qui proposent des schémas de clés de ligne inappropriés, ne traitent pas efficacement le problème de distribution des charges.

question_text:
Your software uses a simple JSON format for all messages. These messages are published to Google Cloud Pub/Sub, then processed with Google Cloud
Dataflow to create a real-time dashboard for the CFO. During testing, you notice that some messages are missing in the dashboard. You check the logs, and all messages are being published to Cloud Pub/Sub successfully. What should you do next?
choice_A: Check the dashboard application to see if it is not displaying correctly.
choice_B: Run a fixed dataset through the Cloud Dataflow pipeline and analyze the output.
choice_C: Use Google Stackdriver Monitoring on Cloud Pub/Sub to find the missing messages.
choice_D: Switch Cloud Dataflow to pull messages from Cloud Pub/Sub instead of Cloud Pub/Sub pushing messages to Cloud Dataflow.
explanation: La réponse correcte est B. Exécuter un jeu de données fixe à travers le pipeline Cloud Dataflow et analyser la sortie. Cela permet de vérifier si le problème réside dans le traitement des données par Cloud Dataflow. En utilisant un jeu de données fixe, vous pouvez isoler et identifier les étapes du pipeline où les messages pourraient être perdus ou mal traités. Les autres options, comme A, C, et D, ne ciblent pas directement le processus de traitement des données dans Cloud Dataflow, qui est crucial pour diagnostiquer pourquoi les messages n'apparaissent pas dans le tableau de bord en temps réel.

question_text:
Flowlogistic Case Study -

Company Overview -
Flowlogistic is a leading logistics and supply chain provider. They help businesses throughout the world manage their resources and transport them to their final destination. The company has grown rapidly, expanding their offerings to include rail, truck, aircraft, and oceanic shipping.

Company Background -
The company started as a regional trucking company, and then expanded into other logistics market. Because they have not updated their infrastructure, managing and tracking orders and shipments has become a bottleneck. To improve operations, Flowlogistic developed proprietary technology for tracking shipments in real time at the parcel level. However, they are unable to deploy it because their technology stack, based on Apache Kafka, cannot support the processing volume. In addition, Flowlogistic wants to further analyze their orders and shipments to determine how best to deploy their resources.

Solution Concept -
Flowlogistic wants to implement two concepts using the cloud:
✑ Use their proprietary technology in a real-time inventory-tracking system that indicates the location of their loads
✑ Perform analytics on all their orders and shipment logs, which contain both structured and unstructured data, to determine how best to deploy resources, which markets to expand info. They also want to use predictive analytics to learn earlier when a shipment will be delayed.

Existing Technical Environment -
Flowlogistic architecture resides in a single data center:
✑ Databases
8 physical servers in 2 clusters
- SQL Server `" user data, inventory, static data
3 physical servers
- Cassandra `" metadata, tracking messages
10 Kafka servers `" tracking message aggregation and batch insert
✑ Application servers `" customer front end, middleware for order/customs
60 virtual machines across 20 physical servers
- Tomcat `" Java services
- Nginx `" static content
- Batch servers
✑ Storage appliances
- iSCSI for virtual machine (VM) hosts
- Fibre Channel storage area network (FC SAN) `" SQL server storage
- Network-attached storage (NAS) image storage, logs, backups
✑ 10 Apache Hadoop /Spark servers
- Core Data Lake
- Data analysis workloads
✑ 20 miscellaneous servers
- Jenkins, monitoring, bastion hosts,

Business Requirements -
Build a reliable and reproducible environment with scaled panty of production.
//IMG//

✑ Aggregate data in a centralized Data Lake for analysis
✑ Use historical data to perform predictive analytics on future shipments
✑ Accurately track every shipment worldwide using proprietary technology
✑ Improve business agility and speed of innovation through rapid provisioning of new resources
✑ Analyze and optimize architecture for performance in the cloud
✑ Migrate fully to the cloud if all other requirements are met

Technical Requirements -
✑ Handle both streaming and batch data
✑ Migrate existing Hadoop workloads
✑ Ensure architecture is scalable and elastic to meet the changing demands of the company.
✑ Use managed services whenever possible
✑ Encrypt data flight and at rest
✑ Connect a VPN between the production data center and cloud environment

SEO Statement -
We have grown so quickly that our inability to upgrade our infrastructure is really hampering further growth and efficiency. We are efficient at moving shipments around the world, but we are inefficient at moving data around.
We need to organize our information so we can more easily understand where our customers are and what they are shipping.

CTO Statement -
IT has never been a priority for us, so as our data has grown, we have not invested enough in our technology. I have a good staff to manage IT, but they are so busy managing our infrastructure that I cannot get them to do the things that really matter, such as organizing our data, building the analytics, and figuring out how to implement the CFO' s tracking technology.

CFO Statement -
Part of our competitive advantage is that we penalize ourselves for late shipments and deliveries. Knowing where out shipments are at all times has a direct correlation to our bottom line and profitability. Additionally, I don't want to commit capital to building out a server environment.
Flowlogistic wants to use Google BigQuery as their primary analysis system, but they still have Apache Hadoop and Spark workloads that they cannot move to
BigQuery. Flowlogistic does not know how to store the data that is common to both workloads. What should they do?
choice_A: Store the common data in BigQuery as partitioned tables.
choice_B: Store the common data in BigQuery and expose authorized views.
choice_C: Store the common data encoded as Avro in Google Cloud Storage.
choice_D: Store he common data in the HDFS storage for a Google Cloud Dataproc cluster.
explanation: La réponse correcte est C. Stocker les données communes encodées en Avro dans Google Cloud Storage. Google Cloud Storage est une solution de stockage évolutive et économique qui permet de stocker des données à la fois pour les charges de travail BigQuery et Hadoop/Spark. En utilisant le format Avro, qui est un format de sérialisation de données efficace et compatible avec Hadoop, Flowlogistic peut facilement intégrer et partager les données entre les différents systèmes d'analyse. Les autres options, telles que le stockage des données uniquement dans BigQuery (A et B) ou dans HDFS pour un cluster Dataproc (D), ne fournissent pas la même flexibilité et interopérabilité entre les environnements BigQuery et Hadoop/Spark.

You need to build a new system on Google Cloud Platform (GCP) that will feed the proprietary tracking software. The system must be able to ingest data from a variety of global sources, process and query in real-time, and store the data reliably. Which combination of GCP products should you choose?
choice_A: Cloud Pub/Sub, Cloud Dataflow, and Cloud Storage

Les autres options, comme l'utilisation de Local SSD ou Cloud SQL, ne sont pas aussi adaptées pour le traitement de flux de données en temps réel et le stockage à grande échelle.

Flowlogistic's CEO wants to gain rapid insight into their customer base so his sales team can be better informed in the field. This team is not very technical, so they've purchased a visualization tool to simplify the creation of BigQuery reports. However, they've been overwhelmed by all the data in the table, and are spending a lot of money on queries trying to find the data they need. You want to solve their problem in the most cost-effective way. What should you do?
choice_A: Export the data into a Google Sheet for virtualization.
choice_B: Create an additional table with only the necessary columns.
choice_C: Create a view on the table to present to the virtualization tool.
choice_D: Create identity and access management (IAM) roles on the appropriate columns, so only they appear in a query.

Une vue permet de sélectionner et de présenter uniquement les colonnes nécessaires sans dupliquer les données, ce qui réduit le coût des requêtes en limitant la quantité de données traitées. Contrairement à l'exportation des données dans une feuille Google (réponse A) ou à la création d'une table supplémentaire (réponse B), une vue n'entraîne pas de stockage supplémentaire ni de gestion complexe. L'utilisation de rôles IAM pour masquer des colonnes (réponse D) n'est pas conçue pour simplifier les requêtes, mais plutôt pour gérer l'accès aux données sensibles.

Flowlogistic is rolling out their real-time inventory tracking system. The tracking devices will all send package-tracking messages, which will now go to a single
Google Cloud Pub/Sub topic instead of the Apache Kafka cluster. A subscriber application will then process the messages for real-time reporting and store them in
Google BigQuery for historical analysis. You want to ensure the package data can be analyzed over time.
Which approach should you take?
choice_A: Attach the timestamp on each message in the Cloud Pub/Sub subscriber application as they are received.
choice_B: Attach the timestamp and Package ID on the outbound message from each publisher device as they are sent to Clod Pub/Sub.
choice_C: Use the NOW () function in BigQuery to record the event's time.
choice_D: Use the automatically generated timestamp from Cloud Pub/Sub to order the data.
explanation: La réponse correcte est B. Attacher l'horodatage et l'ID de colis au message sortant de chaque appareil émetteur lorsqu'ils sont envoyés à Cloud Pub/Sub. Contrairement à l'option A, qui ajoute l'horodatage à la réception, cette méthode évite les décalages temporels potentiels dus à la latence du réseau. L'option C, qui utilise la fonction NOW() dans BigQuery, pourrait ne pas refléter le moment exact de l'événement, et l'option D, qui repose sur l'horodatage généré automatiquement par Cloud Pub/Sub, ne garantit pas que l'ID de colis soit inclus pour une identification précise.

question_text:
MJTelco Case Study -

Company Overview -
MJTelco is a startup that plans to build networks in rapidly growing, underserved markets around the world. The company has patents for innovative optical communications hardware. Based on these patents, they can create many reliable, high-speed backbone links with inexpensive hardware.

Company Background -
Founded by experienced telecom executives, MJTelco uses technologies originally developed to overcome communications challenges in space. Fundamental to their operation, they need to create a distributed data infrastructure that drives real-time analysis and incorporates machine learning to continuously optimize their topologies. Because their hardware is inexpensive, they plan to overdeploy the network allowing them to account for the impact of dynamic regional politics on location availability and cost.
Their management and operations teams are situated all around the globe creating many-to-many relationship between data consumers and provides in their system. After careful consideration, they decided public cloud is the perfect environment to support their needs.

Solution Concept -
MJTelco is running a successful proof-of-concept (PoC) project in its labs. They have two primary needs:
✑ Scale and harden their PoC to support significantly more data flows generated when they ramp to more than 50,000 installations.
✑ Refine their machine-learning cycles to verify and improve the dynamic models they use to control topology definition.
MJTelco will also use three separate operating environments `" development/test, staging, and production `" to meet the needs of running experiments, deploying new features, and serving production customers.

Business Requirements -
✑ Scale up their production environment with minimal cost, instantiating resources when and where needed in an unpredictable, distributed telecom user community.
✑ Ensure security of their proprietary data to protect their leading-edge machine learning and analysis.
✑ Provide reliable and timely access to data for analysis from distributed research workers
✑ Maintain isolated environments that support rapid iteration of their machine-learning models without affecting their customers.

✑ Ensure secure and efficient transport and storage of telemetry data
✑ Rapidly scale instances to support between 10,000 and 100,000 data providers with multiple flows each.
✑ Allow analysis and presentation against data tables tracking up to 2 years of data storing approximately 100m records/day
✑ Support rapid iteration of monitoring infrastructure focused on awareness of data pipeline problems both in telemetry flows and in production learning cycles.

CEO Statement -
Our business model relies on our patents, analytics and dynamic machine learning. Our inexpensive hardware is organized to be highly reliable, which gives us cost advantages. We need to quickly stabilize our large distributed data pipelines to meet our reliability and capacity commitments.

CTO Statement -
Our public cloud services must operate as advertised. We need resources that scale and keep our data secure. We also need environments in which our data scientists can carefully study and quickly adapt our models. Because we rely on automation to process our data, we also need our development and test environments to work as we iterate.

The project is too large for us to maintain the hardware and software required for the data and analysis. Also, we cannot afford to staff an operations team to monitor so many data feeds, so we will rely on automation and infrastructure. MJTelco's Google Cloud Dataflow pipeline is now ready to start receiving data from the 50,000 installations. You want to allow Cloud Dataflow to scale its compute power up as required. Which Cloud Dataflow pipeline configuration setting should you update?
choice_A: The zone
choice_B: The number of workers
choice_C: The disk size per worker
choice_D: The maximum number of workers
explanation: La réponse correcte est D. Le nombre maximum de travailleurs. Dans Google Cloud Dataflow, la capacité de traitement peut être ajustée dynamiquement en modifiant le nombre maximum de travailleurs. Cela permet à la pipeline de s'adapter aux besoins de calcul en augmentant le nombre de travailleurs lorsque la charge de données augmente, ce qui est essentiel pour gérer les flux de données provenant de 50 000 installations. Also, we cannot afford to staff an operations team to monitor so many data feeds, so we will rely on automation and infrastructure. You need to compose visualizations for operations teams with the following requirements:
✑ The report must include telemetry data from all 50,000 installations for the most resent 6 weeks (sampling once every minute).
✑ The report must not be more than 3 hours delayed from live data.
✑ The actionable report should only show suboptimal links.
✑ Most suboptimal links should be sorted to the top.
✑ Suboptimal links can be grouped and filtered by regional geography.
✑ User response time to load the report must be <5 seconds.
Which approach meets the requirements?
choice_A: Load the data into Google Sheets, use formulas to calculate a metric, and use filters/sorting to show only suboptimal links in a table.
choice_B: Load the data into Google BigQuery tables, write Google Apps Script that queries the data, calculates the metric, and shows only suboptimal rows in a table in Google Sheets.
choice_C: Load the data into Google Cloud Datastore tables, write a Google App Engine Application that queries all rows, applies a function to derive the metric, and then renders results in a table using the Google charts and visualization API.
choice_D: Load the data into Google BigQuery tables, write a Google Data Studio 360 report that connects to your data, calculates a metric, and then uses a filter expression to show only suboptimal rows in a table.
explanation: La réponse correcte est D. Charger les données dans les tables Google BigQuery et créer un rapport Google Data Studio 360 qui se connecte aux données, calcule un indicateur, et utilise une expression de filtre pour afficher uniquement les lignes sous-optimales dans un tableau. Cette approche est idéale car BigQuery est conçu pour gérer de grandes quantités de données et permet des requêtes rapides, ce qui est essentiel pour respecter le délai de chargement de moins de 5 secondes. De plus, Google Data Studio offre des capacités de visualisation avancées et peut facilement filtrer et trier les liens sous-optimaux, répondant ainsi aux exigences de regroupement et de filtrage géographique. Les autres choix, comme A, B, et C, ne sont pas aussi efficaces pour traiter et visualiser des volumes de données aussi importants en temps quasi réel.

question_text: You create an important report for your large team in Google Data Studio 360. The report uses Google BigQuery as its data source. You notice that visualizations are not showing data that is less than 1 hour old. What should you do?
choice_A: Disable caching by editing the report settings.
choice_B: Disable caching in BigQuery by editing table details.
choice_C: Refresh your browser tab showing the visualizations.
choice_D: Clear your browser history for the past hour then reload the tab showing the virtualizations.
explanation: La réponse correcte est A. Désactiver la mise en cache en modifiant les paramètres du rapport. Dans Google Data Studio, la mise en cache est utilisée pour améliorer les performances en stockant temporairement les données récupérées. Cela peut entraîner un décalage dans l'affichage des données récentes, comme celles de moins d'une heure. En désactivant la mise en cache dans les paramètres du rapport, vous forcez Data Studio à récupérer les données directement depuis BigQuery à chaque fois, assurant ainsi que les visualisations affichent les informations les plus récentes. Les autres options, comme B, C, et D, ne résoudraient pas le problème de mise à jour des données en temps réel.

You create a new report for your large team in Google Data Studio 360. The report uses Google BigQuery as its data source. It is company policy to ensure employees can view only the data associated with their region, so you create and populate a table for each region. You need to enforce the regional access policy to the data.
Which two actions should you take? (Choose two.)
choice_A: Ensure all the tables are included in global dataset.
choice_B: Ensure each table is included in a dataset for a region.
choice_C: Adjust the settings for each table to allow a related region-based security group view access.
